title: Quiz: Applications of Quantum Reservoir Computing
description: Test your understanding of QRC applications across different domains

questions.question: Why is QRC particularly well-suited for financial time series prediction?

options.text: High-dimensional feature space captures complex correlations

questions.explanation: QRC's high-dimensional quantum feature space can capture complex correlations between multiple assets and temporal patterns that simpler models might miss. This makes it suitable for modeling financial market dynamics.

questions.question: What adjustment should be made to the leaking rate for highly chaotic systems?

options.text: Use very low leaking rate for maximum memory
Use higher leaking rate to match faster dynamics
Leaking rate doesn't matter for chaotic systems

questions.explanation: Chaotic systems have fast dynamics characterized by positive Lyapunov exponents. A higher leaking rate allows the reservoir to track these rapid changes more effectively.

questions.question: In QRC-based noise cancellation, what does the filter learn to estimate?

options.text: The noise component to be subtracted

questions.explanation: In noise cancellation, the QRC filter learns to estimate the noise component from a reference signal. This estimated noise is then subtracted from the primary signal to recover the clean signal.

questions.question: For sequence classification, what features are typically extracted from the reservoir?

options.text: Final state combined with temporal statistics

questions.explanation: For classification, features typically combine the final reservoir state (encoding the full sequence history) with statistics like mean and variance over time, capturing both the sequence endpoint and its dynamics.

questions.question: QRC can be used to characterize unknown quantum systems by learning their dynamics from measurement data.
questions.explanation: True. This is a uniquely quantum application where QRC learns to predict the evolution of a target quantum system from its measurement time series, effectively modeling its dynamics without explicit knowledge of its Hamiltonian.

questions.question: What type of problems is QRC most suitable for?

options.text: Temporal and sequential data with complex patterns

questions.explanation: QRC excels at temporal and sequential data processing where the high-dimensional quantum feature space and natural dynamics can capture complex temporal patterns that simpler methods struggle with.

questions.question: What is an advantage of QRC for problems with limited training data?

options.text: The reservoir provides a rich feature space without training
QRC ignores data and makes random predictions

questions.explanation: The quantum reservoir provides a rich, high-dimensional feature transformation without requiring training of the reservoir itself. Only the simple linear readout needs training, which requires less data than training deep networks.

questions.question: Which applications are good candidates for QRC? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: QRC is suitable for: (a) speech recognition due to temporal patterns, (c) chaotic prediction leveraging high-dimensional features, and (d) adaptive filtering using temporal memory. Sorting is a non-temporal combinatorial problem not suited for QRC.
